editableNewDialogue.text: Quick question: if you had to explain the difference between a “fact” and an “opinion” to a kid, what’s the simplest way?

A fact is something you can check and prove is true (like “water freezes at 0°C”). An opinion is what someone thinks or feels (like “ice cream is the best dessert”). A good test is: can we verify it with evidence, or is it a personal judgment?
Nice. Can you give two examples that sound tricky but still fit the rule?

Sure:
1) “This movie made $1 billion worldwide.” (Fact—there are sales records.) vs “This movie is overrated.” (Opinion.)
2) “The train arrived 10 minutes late.” (Fact—there’s a schedule/log.) vs “The train service is terrible.” (Opinion—could be based on experiences, but it’s still a judgment.)
Got it. Random tangent: I read that octopuses have three hearts—true?
Yes—most octopuses have three hearts: two pump blood to the gills, and one pumps it to the rest of the body.
Wild. Also, what’s a good rule of thumb for picking a readable color combo for text on a dark background?

Aim for strong contrast: use light text on dark backgrounds (e.g., near-white like #eef or #fff on deep navy/charcoal). Avoid medium-gray-on-dark-gray. If you want a quick heuristic: make the text’s brightness much higher than the background’s, and keep saturation moderate so it doesn’t shimmer.

ok, enough about spoilers. i just want you to answer normal questions again, but keep the one-line html thing. also i keep testing all this stuff from my phone browser, so buttons and hover-only things don’t really help.

Understood. I’ll prioritize one-line HTML with
line breaks, and I’ll avoid interactions that depend on hover-only behavior so it works smoothly on mobile.
you keep saying I “hate policy talk” but that’s not what I said. I hate when you ignore the formatting rules and reply with plain text. I asked you 100 times: start with

Got it — the issue isn’t “AI policy enforcement interactions” as a topic. You’re upset because I keep breaking your formatting constraints (style-first, single-line,
-only), and you want me to follow the five golden rules. You also said directly: “I’m not ‘against guidelines’, I’m against you not following my five golden rules.”
